body:
# Get help
Summary


Base UI Input (which renders Field.Control) triggers React state updates on each keystroke (setDirty, setFilled), causing the input component/subtree to re-render continuously while typing. This happens even when the input is used in an uncontrolled way via React Hook Form register.

This makes Base UI Input hard to use in performance-sensitive forms, because RHF’s usual “uncontrolled input = minimal rerenders” benefit is lost.

Expected behavior


When using an uncontrolled input (no value prop, only ref/onChange/onBlur from RHF register), typing should not force React re-renders of the input subtree on every keystroke unless the consumer explicitly opts into controlled state or subscriptions.

Actual behavior


Typing into Base UI Input triggers re-renders on every keystroke.

## Ask a question

Request / questions

1. Is there an intended way to use Base UI Input in a mode that avoids per-keystroke React state updates (and therefore avoids re-rendering the control)?

2. Would you consider an opt-out for tracking dirty/filled/focused state (e.g. trackDirty, trackFilled, trackFocus) for integrations where an external form library owns state/validation?

3. Alternatively, could Field.Control avoid updating React state on each input event and instead update some of this state on blur/commit, or compute it from DOM without setting state?
title: @base-ui/react/input (Field.Control) re-renders on every keystroke even when used uncontrolled (e.g. React Hook Form register)
